2606.30237.title: Comparing Human and Automatic Recognition of Dutch Dysarthric Continuous Speech: A Case Study
2606.30237.paper_summary: In our goal to develop personalised dysarthric speech recognition (DSR) models, this study compared the recognition performances of human listeners and those of three state-of-the-art, off-the-shelf ASR systems (Whisper-large-V3, Google Chirp 3, and Omnilingual) on the recognition of Dutch continuous read and spontaneous speech from a single speaker with severe dysarthria. Results showed that both humans listeners and the three off-the-shelf ASR systems exhibit word error rates (WER) exceeding 70% on average, indicating that DSR is highly challenging for both humans and ASR systems. Fine-tuning on the dysarthric speech significantly reduced WER. Although overall WERs are still quite high (>23%), the personalised DSR models outperformed the human listeners, and performance is getting closer to being useful for supporting day-to-day communication of dysarthric speakers. Future research should focus on improving personalized DSR on spontaneous speech and longer utterances in the case of read speech, with a specific focus on particular phonemes.
2606.30237.paper_summary_zh: 在我們開發個性化的口吃語音識別（DSR）模型的目標下，本研究比較了人類聽眾與三種最先進的現成自動語音識別（ASR）系統（Whisper-large-V3、Google Chirp 3 和 Omnilingual）在識別來自一位重度口吃者的荷蘭語連續朗讀和自發性語音的表現。結果顯示，人類聽眾和這三種現成的 ASR 系統的單詞錯誤率（WER）平均均超過 70%，這表明 DSR 對於人類和 ASR 系統而言都是高度具有挑戰性的。對口吃語音的微調顯著降低了 WER。儘管整體 WER 仍然相當高（>23%），但個性化的 DSR 模型的表現超過了人類聽眾，並且其表現越來越接近於能夠支持口吃者的日常溝通。未來的研究應專注於改善自發性語音和朗讀語音中較長語句的個性化 DSR，特別關注特定的音素。
2606.30237.author: Yuanyuan Zhang et.al.
2606.30237.authors: Yuanyuan Zhang, Dimme de Groot, Jorge Martinez, Odette Scharenborg
